    1. create(Class)

Class RequestFactory

java.lang.Object
com.habbashx.proxy.factory.RequestFactory
public class RequestFactory extends Object
Public entry point of the framework — assembles all components and produces a live proxy for a given API class.

Calling create(Class) wires together the full object graph:

  1. A MethodCache for lazy annotation metadata parsing.
  2. A HttpClient and RequestRegistry for HTTP dispatch.
  3. A JacksonSerializer for JSON (de)serialization.
  4. An InterceptorHierarchy containing HeaderInterceptor and PathQueryInterceptor, applied in that order.
  5. A RequestProxyEngine as the Byte Buddy interception target.
  6. A Byte Buddy-generated subclass of apiClass that delegates every method call to the engine.

Usage: 

UserApi api = RequestFactory.create(UserApi.class);
User user   = api.getUser(42);

This class is not instantiable; use the static create method.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type

    Description
    static <T> T
    create(Class<T> apiClass)
    Creates a proxy instance for the given API class with the default configuration.

  • Method Details

    • create

      @NotNull public static <T> T create(Class<T> apiClass) throws ReflectiveOperationException
      Creates a proxy instance for the given API class with the default configuration.

      The returned object is a Byte Buddy-generated subclass of apiClass loaded into a child class loader via ClassLoadingStrategy.Default.WRAPPER. Every method call on the returned instance is intercepted and routed through the request pipeline.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the API type
      Parameters:
      apiClass - the class (or abstract class) whose methods should be proxied; must have a public no-arg constructor
      Returns:
      a fully wired proxy instance ready to make HTTP calls
      Throws:
      ReflectiveOperationException - if the proxy class cannot be instantiated
